patch.value: Pre-install package-owned command names should hint the owning package install command

2026-07-04 sandbox audit: in a fresh workspace, pm completion/guide/templates fail with generic unknown-command guidance (examples: pm --help, pm activity --help). These names are known package-owned surfaces (pm-guide-shell, pm-templates); unknown-command guidance should map known first-party package command roots to a hint like: install with pm install guide-shell --project. Keeps never-block promise for the pre-install experience.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Fixed same session: KNOWN_PACKAGE_COMMAND_HINTS extended from 4 entries (guide/templates/calendar/cal) to all package-owned command roots — completion + completion-statuses/tags/types + shell (guide-shell), comments-audit/dedupe-audit/dedupe-merge/normalize (governance-audit), reindex/search-advanced (search-advanced), test-runs (linked-test-adapters). Aliases verified against packages/*/package.json pm.aliases.

patch.value: Every package-owned command root (completion*, shell, guide, templates, calendar/cal, comments-audit, dedupe-audit, dedupe-merge, normalize, reindex, search-advanced, test-runs) yields an install hint naming the owning package pre-install.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-04 verified live in fresh workspace: guide/shell/completion/templates/calendar/reindex/test-runs/dedupe-audit/normalize all yield owning-package install hints pre-install. Also deduped the doubled install step (commander-usage generic hint vs specific hint) in buildKnownPackageCommandGuidance.

patch.value:
## Context
2026-07-04 sandbox audit: in a fresh workspace, `pm completion`, `pm guide`, `pm templates` fail with generic unknown-command guidance (suggesting pm --help / pm activity --help) even though these are known first-party package-owned command roots (pm-guide-shell, pm-templates). Pre-install UX breaks the never-block promise.

## Approach
Maintain a static map of first-party package-owned command roots → owning package alias (derivable from the bundled catalog, same source as pm-jqd2's alias matching). When unknown-command dispatch misses, check the map first and emit: "command 'guide' is provided by the guide-shell package — install with: pm install guide-shell --project".

## Definition of done
Fresh workspace: every bundled package-owned root command name yields the owning install hint; unknown non-package names keep the current did-you-mean; hint catalog covered by spec (install-hint catalog was 4→16 in the 2026-07-04 audit — reuse it).

## Context
2026-07-04 sandbox review: in a fresh workspace, `pm completion`, `pm guide`, `pm templates` fail with generic unknown-command guidance (suggesting pm --help / pm activity --help) even though these are known first-party package-owned command roots (pm-guide-shell, pm-templates). Pre-install UX breaks the never-block promise.

## Approach
Maintain a static map of first-party package-owned command roots → owning package alias (derivable from the bundled catalog, same source as pm-jqd2's alias matching). When unknown-command dispatch misses, check the map first and emit: "command 'guide' is provided by the guide-shell package — install with: pm install guide-shell --project".

## Definition of done
Fresh workspace: every bundled package-owned root command name yields the owning install hint; unknown non-package names keep the current did-you-mean; hint catalog covered by spec (install-hint catalog was 4→16 in the 2026-07-04 review — reuse it).

These names are known package-owned surfaces (pm-guide-shell, pm-templates); unknown-command guidance should map known first-party package command roots to a hint like: install with pm install guide-shell --project. Keeps never-block promise for the pre-install experience.

value.author: codex-root-20260709
value.text: Verification evidence 2026-07-10: full sandbox suite passed 270 files / 5,505 tests with exact 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines. pm test-all over the seven active items passed 8 linked commands with 0 failures and 1 duplicate-safe skip. pnpm typecheck, contracts:check, lint (ESLint + 0 jscpd clones + static quality/token budget), docs-skills, context-eval, security scan, and audit all passed. Live CodeFactor reports A+ and 0 issues. Fresh packed npm install at /tmp/pm-cli-sdk-path-proof-XIsSsh activated 11 bundled packages in a path-target tracker, left caller extension files at zero, and passed CLI/SDK context plus isolated doctor with zero blocking failures. pm health is clean and history drift is zero.

patch.value: Verified package-owned command guidance maps unavailable first-party commands to their install commands.
Pre-install unknown commands identify the owning package and recovery command.
Focused error-guidance linked tests pass in the full active-item verification bundle.
Implementation, docs, contracts, exact coverage, linked tests, and packed installed-package proof complete.
